title: The legislation challenging racial bias in court cases would ensure the recently-signed Racial Justice Act can be applied retroactively for all Californians
prompt_preview:

Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California Racial Justice Act prohibits the use of race, ethnicity, or national origin in sentencing and convictions.",
    "The California Racial Justice Act can be applied retroactively for all Californians.",
    "Racial bias in the criminal legal system is pervasive and pernicious.",
    "The California Racial Justice Act provides people the opportunity to challenge racial bias in the criminal legal process if it occurs after January 1, 2021.",
    "The Racial Justice Act was established in response to the 1987 legal precedent set by McCleskey v. Kemp.",
    "The McCleskey decision requires criminal defendants to prove intentional discrimination when challenging racial bias.",
    "The high standard established by McCleskey v. Kemp hinders legal remedies for racial bias in criminal cases.",
    "Statistical evidence shows systemic bias in charging and sentencing in California's criminal justice system.",
    "Four out of every ten people incarcerated in California state prison are African American men.",
    "The imprisonment rate for African American men in California is ten times that of white men."
  ]
}
```
